title: Cardamomum capitatum
taxonomy: Cardamomum capitatum is classified in the family Zingiberaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the ginger family. This family includes over 1,500 species of plants, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. The genus Cardamomum is further divided into several species, including Cardamomum capitatum. The exact classification of Cardamomum capitatum may also include other levels of classification, such as kingdom, order, and class. The classification of Cardamomum capitatum is subject to ongoing research and revision by taxonomists.
uses: Cardamomum capitatum is known for its various medicinal properties and uses, primarily in traditional medicine. Its leaves and roots are used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. side_effects: Cardamomum capitatum, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, is not the same as the commonly known Cardamomum, however the plant Cardamomum capitatum is known to have some side effects.
